ticket.subject: Database performance degradation after upgrading to MySQL 8.0 from 5.7
ticket.body: We migrated from MySQL 5.7 to 8.0 three days ago. A JOIN query on our orders table (120M rows) with a composite B-tree index is now executing in 3.8s p99 compared to 0.6s previously. EXPLAIN output indicates the query optimizer is choosing a full table scan instead of the index. Could this be related to histogram statistics or cost model changes in 8.0? Do we need to rebuild statistics manually?
